LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS-DEFENDANTS KATHERINE ROBERTS AND ROBERTS MANAGEMENT'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT. RULING: NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. CROSS-COMPLAINT IS VAGUE AND UNCERTAIN AS TO ALL CAUSES OF ACTION. CROSS-COMPLAINANT MUST IDENTIFY WHICH CROSS-DEFENDANT WAS THE LANDLORD OR LESSOR OF THE PROPERTY, DESIGNATE WHICH CAUSES OF ACTION RELATE TO WHICH CROSS-DEFENDANT, AND SPECIFY FACTS THAT SUPPORT EACH CAUSE OF ACTION AGAINST THOSE CROSS-DEFENDANTS. CROSS-COMPLAINANT MAY NOT SUPPORT HER CLAIMS WITH CONCLUSORY ALLEGATIONS. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-15-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: CROSS DEFENDANT KATHERINE ROBERTS, AND, ROBERTS MANAGEMENT DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Appearances: Jeffrey V. Ta, Esq. of Bledsore, Cathcart, Diestel, Pedersen & Treppa LLP (415) 981-5411, appearing for Cross Defendant and moving party; George M. Rush, Esq. of the Law Offices of George Rush, (415) 393-8005, appearing for Defendant Edison. Argued and the Court adopted and modified its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer is overruled on all counts, except the Court sustains the second cause of action, (warranty of habitability cause of action,) ten days leave to answer. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-13-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: CROSS-DEFENDANTS KATHERINE ROBERTS AND ROBERTS MANAGEMENT'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT.
RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. CROSS-COMPLAINT IS VAGUE AND UNCERTAIN AS TO ALL CAUSES OF ACTION. CROSS-COMPLAINANT MUST IDENTIFY WHICH CROSS-DEFENDANT WAS THE LANDLORD OR LESSOR OF THE PROPERTY, DESIGNATE WHICH CAUSES OF ACTION RELATE TO WHICH CROSS-DEFENDANT, AND SPECIFY FACTS THAT SUPPORT EACH CAUSE OF ACTION AGAINST THOSE CROSS-DEFENDANTS. CROSS-COMPLAINANT MAY NOT SUPPORT HER CLAIMS WITH CONCLUSORY ALLEGATIONS. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) (302/CWW/JW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-22-2009 Continued To Jun-26-09 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 212

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-02-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CHERYL EDISON'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Appearances: Philip T. Kilduff, Esq., specially appearing for Plaintiff, and opposing party; There are no other appearances. There is no argument. The Court adopted and amends its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer overruled, with days leave to amend. Plaintiff states a cause of action for common counts. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/CWW/PB)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759
